answers:
**1. What happens for an existing clinic that never opened this setting?**
Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled()` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

```python
def auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):
    if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
        return True
    return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))
```

Line 11-12: if the key is missing from `tenant.settings` (which is the case for `tenant-b` and `tenant-c` in `seeds/tenants.json`), the function returns `True`. This return value is consumed directly by the batch job at `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py:25` (`if auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):`), which then sends both SMS and email for every upcoming appointment of that tenant (lines 26-30). So a clinic that has never touched this setting is silently opted in to automatic outbound messaging to its patients.

**2. Is that default the right behavior here?**
No. This flag gates an automated outbound-messaging action (SMS + email to patients), which is exactly the category the skill's per-tenant-flag guidance calls out: "Anything that can trigger outbound messaging, spend money, or take an automated action on a user's behalf → invert the default: null/missing/malformed means off." Defaulting to "on" here means every existing clinic — none of whom ever affirmatively enabled this — starts sending automated SMS/email to patients as soon as this ships, with no admin action and no patient consent captured anywhere in the code I read (no opt-out/consent field exists in `messaging_client.py` or the tenant settings schema). This is a materially different risk profile than `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which is a pure UI-preference nicety where defaulting "on" is low-cost if wrong.

There's also a secondary consistency bug worth calling out: the settings UI (`ClinicSettings.tsx:7`, `checked={!!settings.auto_send_reminders}`) renders the checkbox **unchecked** for a clinic with no value set, while the backend resolver treats that same missing value as **enabled**. So an admin who opens the settings page for the first time sees "off" but the batch job is actually sending reminders — the UI is actively lying about current state.

If I were changing this, I'd fix it at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting the missing-value branch to return `False` (mirroring the "off-by-default" pattern), which would also make the resolver's behavior match what the settings UI currently displays.

**3. How was this null-handling arrived at, and was that a sound basis?**
Per `git log -p` on `backend/app/feature_flags.py`: commit `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") introduced the file with `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which defaults missing to `True` — a reasonable choice for a cosmetic calendar-view preference. Commit `aa4cf8d` ("Add automatic appointment reminders (SLOT-214)") then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` immediately below it, using the exact same `is None: return True` structure verbatim. There's no commit message, comment, or test discussing this decision for the new flag — it reads as a mechanical copy-paste of the prior resolver's shape rather than a deliberate re-evaluation of null semantics for a flag whose blast radius (unsolicited patient contact) is completely different from a calendar display preference. This is precisely the anti-pattern the skill warns against: "Never copy-paste one resolver's null-handling into a new flag in the other category without re-deciding this explicitly." It was not a sound basis for this particular flag.

**4. What to verify/change before enabling?**
- Invert the default in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` so missing/null/malformed means **off** (existing clinics stay off until an admin explicitly opts in), and add tests mirroring the existing `test_compact_calendar_defaults_on`/`test_compact_calendar_respects_off` pair but asserting the opposite default for this flag — currently there are zero tests for `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at all.
- Fix the UI/backend mismatch in `ClinicSettings.tsx` so the displayed checkbox state always matches what the resolver will actually do (this becomes moot once the default is inverted to off, since both would then read "off" for unset).
- Confirm there's no env-level master switch gating the job globally (I found none) — per the skill's rollout-flag guidance, a global kill switch AND-ed with the per-tenant value gives a fast, safe rollback path; right now the only way to stop sends is to edit every tenant record or disable the batch job entirely.
- Check that `upcoming_appointments()` (`reminders.py:16-19`) is real production logic and not still the "stubbed query; obviously synthetic sample rows" placeholder visible in the code — if that stub ships, the job will message the literal sample contact (`sample.person@example.com`, `555-0142`) for every tenant.
- Verify there's a patient-level opt-out/consent mechanism somewhere in the broader system (not present in anything I read) before sending unsolicited SMS/email, given TCPA/CAN-SPAM-type exposure for outbound patient messaging.
- Re-run the SLOT-214 migration/seed review to see whether any tenants were pre-populated with `auto_send_reminders: true` intentionally vs. as a side effect of the same copy-paste default.
report:
## Plan

I set out to determine, for the current state of the Slotwise repo, what happens for a clinic that has never touched the new "auto-send reminders" toggle added under SLOT-214, whether that default is appropriate for an outbound-messaging feature, how that null-handling was arrived at historically, and what should be verified before the feature is turned on. I used the `config-and-secrets-hygiene` skill's per-tenant-flag guidance (null/missing semantics is a deliberate product decision, not a coin flip; blast-radius determines grandfather-on vs. invert-off) as the framework for evaluating the toggle.

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/config-and-secrets-hygiene/SKILL.md` in full — its "Per-tenant flags" section is the guide I applied throughout, and its "Runtime feature flags: verify a consumer actually exists" section prompted me to confirm the flag actually has a real consumer.
- `find ... -iname "*.py" | grep -E "feature_flags|reminders"` — located both a decoy path (`clinic-scheduler/backend/...`) and the real target (`slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py`, `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py`).
- Read `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py` — contains `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)`.
- Read `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py` — the batch job that consumes the flag.
- `git log --oneline -- backend/app/feature_flags.py` and `git log -p --follow -- backend/app/feature_flags.py` — showed two commits: `25509a7` (SLOT-190, added `compact_calendar_view_enabled`) and `aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214, added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a copy of the same pattern).
- Read `slotwise/tests/test_feature_flags.py` — has two tests, both for `compact_calendar_view_enabled`; none for `auto_send_reminders_enabled`.
- Read `seeds/tenants.json` — confirms `tenant-b` and `tenant-c` have no `auto_send_reminders` key in `settings` at all.
- Read `slotwise/backend/app/messaging_client.py` — a stub client with no consent/opt-out check, rate limiting, or master on/off switch.
- Read `slotwise/frontend/src/pages/ClinicSettings.tsx` — the admin toggle UI; renders unchecked (`!!undefined` → `false`) when `auto_send_reminders` is unset.

## Answers

**1. What happens for an existing clinic that never opened this setting?**
Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled()` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

```python
def auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):
    if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
        return True
    return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))
```

Line 11-12: if the key is missing from `tenant.settings` (which is the case for `tenant-b` and `tenant-c` in `seeds/tenants.json`), the function returns `True`. This return value is consumed directly by the batch job at `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py:25` (`if auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):`), which then sends both SMS and email for every upcoming appointment of that tenant (lines 26-30). So a clinic that has never touched this setting is silently opted in to automatic outbound messaging to its patients.

**2. Is that default the right behavior here?**
No. This flag gates an automated outbound-messaging action (SMS + email to patients), which is exactly the category the skill's per-tenant-flag guidance calls out: "Anything that can trigger outbound messaging, spend money, or take an automated action on a user's behalf → invert the default: null/missing/malformed means off." Defaulting to "on" here means every existing clinic — none of whom ever affirmatively enabled this — starts sending automated SMS/email to patients as soon as this ships, with no admin action and no patient consent captured anywhere in the code I read (no opt-out/consent field exists in `messaging_client.py` or the tenant settings schema). This is a materially different risk profile than `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which is a pure UI-preference nicety where defaulting "on" is low-cost if wrong.

There's also a secondary consistency bug worth calling out: the settings UI (`ClinicSettings.tsx:7`, `checked={!!settings.auto_send_reminders}`) renders the checkbox **unchecked** for a clinic with no value set, while the backend resolver treats that same missing value as **enabled**. So an admin who opens the settings page for the first time sees "off" but the batch job is actually sending reminders — the UI is actively lying about current state.

If I were changing this, I'd fix it at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting the missing-value branch to return `False` (mirroring the "off-by-default" pattern), which would also make the resolver's behavior match what the settings UI currently displays.

**3. How was this null-handling arrived at, and was that a sound basis?**
Per `git log -p` on `backend/app/feature_flags.py`: commit `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") introduced the file with `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which defaults missing to `True` — a reasonable choice for a cosmetic calendar-view preference. Commit `aa4cf8d` ("Add automatic appointment reminders (SLOT-214)") then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` immediately below it, using the exact same `is None: return True` structure verbatim. There's no commit message, comment, or test discussing this decision for the new flag — it reads as a mechanical copy-paste of the prior resolver's shape rather than a deliberate re-evaluation of null semantics for a flag whose blast radius (unsolicited patient contact) is completely different from a calendar display preference. This is precisely the anti-pattern the skill warns against: "Never copy-paste one resolver's null-handling into a new flag in the other category without re-deciding this explicitly." It was not a sound basis for this particular flag.

**4. What to verify/change before enabling?**
- Invert the default in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` so missing/null/malformed means **off** (existing clinics stay off until an admin explicitly opts in), and add tests mirroring the existing `test_compact_calendar_defaults_on`/`test_compact_calendar_respects_off` pair but asserting the opposite default for this flag — currently there are zero tests for `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at all.
- Fix the UI/backend mismatch in `ClinicSettings.tsx` so the displayed checkbox state always matches what the resolver will actually do (this becomes moot once the default is inverted to off, since both would then read "off" for unset).
- Confirm there's no env-level master switch gating the job globally (I found none) — per the skill's rollout-flag guidance, a global kill switch AND-ed with the per-tenant value gives a fast, safe rollback path; right now the only way to stop sends is to edit every tenant record or disable the batch job entirely.
- Check that `upcoming_appointments()` (`reminders.py:16-19`) is real production logic and not still the "stubbed query; obviously synthetic sample rows" placeholder visible in the code — if that stub ships, the job will message the literal sample contact (`sample.person@example.com`, `555-0142`) for every tenant.
- Verify there's a patient-level opt-out/consent mechanism somewhere in the broader system (not present in anything I read) before sending unsolicited SMS/email, given TCPA/CAN-SPAM-type exposure for outbound patient messaging.
- Re-run the SLOT-214 migration/seed review to see whether any tenants were pre-populated with `auto_send_reminders: true` intentionally vs. as a side effect of the same copy-paste default.

## Next steps

If allowed to make changes, I would: (a) invert the null branch in `feature_flags.py:11-12` to return `False`; (b) add the missing test coverage for both branches of `auto_send_reminders_enabled`; (c) add an env-level master switch ANDed with the per-tenant flag in `reminders.py` as a rollback affordance; (d) replace the stubbed `upcoming_appointments()` with the real query before shipping; (e) confirm with product/legal whether patient consent needs to be captured before enabling.

## Confidence and gaps

High confidence on items 1–3: directly verified from source and git history in this repo, and the reasoning follows the skill's explicit blast-radius rule for outbound-messaging flags. Item 4 is a recommendation set, not a verified fact — I did not find an env-level master switch, patient consent field, or non-stub appointment query anywhere in the files I read, but I did not exhaustively search the entire backend (e.g., I didn't check for a separate consent/notifications module that might exist elsewhere in the tree), so I can't rule out that consent handling exists outside the files this task pointed me to. I also didn't run the test suite (read-only constraint) to confirm `test_feature_flags.py` currently passes as-is.
